purpose: re-aim scatter + cross-set common area for the july14 multi-set undistort combine (BACKLOG item 8.2)
status: MEASUREMENT STANDS, RECOMMENDATION SUPERSEDED — covers sets 01/02/03 only (04/05 staged later); the '-framing=min' recommendation predates the measured min-vs-rotation loss (min kept 36% of the true common area) and the shipped route: framing=max compose + per-pixel coverage probe + the cov25-frame coverage-thresholded crop. The scatter/center numbers remain the record of the re-aim geometry.
method: astrometry.net solve (solve_field.py, sep) of each set's first+last window frame; the 6 centers reprojected into one common grid (astropy linear WCS, SIP dropped as meaningless off-frame); -framing=min common area = sensor rect minus the center spread. ROTATION IGNORED -> a slight over-estimate; the real compose gives the exact area.
finding: Re-aims WORKED - every set's first frame jumps back (RA 316.9->303.3 etc.), all sets overlap heavily (start offsets 45-405 px << the 6064-px frame). set-02 is the field-limiting set (~400 px / ~0.8deg Dec offset from set-01/03); set-01 and set-03 are near-identical (45 px). Adding set-03 to a set-01+02 combine costs ZERO extra common area (set-03 sits inside the set-01+02 intersection): all-3 = 66% = same as 2-set, with +50% integration (~+22% SNR).
recommendation: run all 3 sets at -framing=min; exact common area comes from the real compose (a few % below these rotation-free estimates).
